# LLM Integration Report — DungeonExporer

> Word count: ~780 (body).  
> Last updated: 2026-05-15

## 1. Introduction

*DungeonExporer* is a first-person dungeon slice built in Unity 6000.3.8f1. Its distinguishing feature is optional narrative text generated by a **local** large language model through [Ollama](https://ollama.com), bound to `http://localhost:11434`. The game remains fully playable when Ollama is missing: quests, combat, and UI use authored C# content. The LLM adds atmosphere and NPC voice, not core progression logic.

This report summarizes technical decisions, integration strategy, performance considerations, gameplay impact, and how AI tools shaped the development workflow.

## 2. Technical decisions

### 2.1 Why Ollama and a local model

We chose Ollama over cloud APIs for four reasons aligned with the project brief: **no per-token cost** during iteration and playtesting; **offline play** after the model is pulled; **privacy** (prompts stay on the player machine); and **pedagogical clarity** for demonstrating integration in a technical video. The default model is **`qwen3:4b`**, small enough for CPU inference on 16 GB RAM class laptops, with optional **`llama3`** for higher prose quality on stronger GPUs.

### 2.2 Client architecture

Gameplay uses `OllamaHandler` (`Assets/Scripts/OllamaHandler.cs`), which posts to Ollama’s **`/api/generate`** endpoint with UnityWebRequest. NPC dialogue uses **NDJSON streaming** (`stream: true`) so TextMesh Pro can reveal tokens progressively. Zone flavor uses a **non-streaming** call with a lower `num_predict` cap. A separate package, **SimpleOllamaUnity**, is included for future consolidation but is not yet the primary path.

Reasoning models leak planning text; we send **`think: false`** on gameplay requests, sanitize outputs (`SanitizeModelOutput`, `ExtractNpcSpokenDialogue`), and strip known failure patterns (e.g. lines starting with “Quest title:”). An abort path cancels in-flight requests when the dialogue panel closes.

### 2.3 Authoritative game state vs. stateless LLM

The LLM is **stateless between calls**. Each request rebuilds context from:

- `QuestDefinition` (title, briefing — not generated),
- `QuestManager.BuildPromptContext()` (active/completed quests),
- `PlayerInventory.BuildSummaryForPrompt()`,
- `NpcConversationMemory` (last few assistant lines per NPC id).

Quest acceptance, objectives (`defeated_dungeon_foe`, `entered_encounter_zone`), and rewards are **never** parsed from model output. This “facts in code, flavor in model” split limits hallucination risk and keeps marking/replay deterministic.

### 2.4 Boot-time health check

`OllamaFirstRunHealthCheck` queries `/api/tags` and verifies the configured model name exists. Failure opens `OllamaSetupPanelController`, linking to `docs/setup.md`, with **Continue** so players without Ollama can still play. This addresses the highest-likelihood deployment risk (Ollama not installed) without hard-blocking the executable.

## 3. Integration strategy

Two LLM touchpoints ship in Level1:

1. **NPC dialogue** — `NpcInteractable` → `DialoguePanelController` → **Hear them out** → streamed Cap lines.
2. **Environmental flavor** — `DungeonFlavorZone` → `DungeonFlavorNarrator` → HUD toast on safe/encounter tiles.

Both respect UI gates (`NarrationUiGate`) so prompts do not fire during pause or dialogue. Flavor uses a **cooldown** (~42 s) to avoid spamming the server and the player.

Prompt templates are documented in `docs/prompts-used.md`. Development iterated from a verbose “authoritative quest” prompt (which caused qwen3 to echo instructions) to a shorter template ending with `Cap: "` to force in-character completion.

Options menu exposes **`GameSettings.LlmEnabled`**; when false, NPC requests show a canned line and flavor narration is skipped — supporting players who disable AI or lack hardware headroom.

## 4. Performance considerations

Targets in `docs/ollama-plan.md` aim for sub–5 s P95 for short lines on recommended hardware. In practice:

- **First request** after launch incurs model load latency (Ollama cold start).
- **Streaming** improves perceived latency: text appears before generation finishes.
- **Token caps** (`num_predict` ~72 flavor, ~180 dialogue stream) bound worst-case stall.
- Work runs on **coroutines**; the main thread only appends UI strings.

Future work: menu **warm-up** prompt, measured benchmarks in `docs/eval/`, and honoring `LlmEnabled` inside `OllamaHandler` for a single choke point.

## 5. Gameplay impact

The LLM supports the **cosy fantasy** tone described in `docs/high-concept.md`. Static quest text stays readable for assessment; optional AI lines reward players who install Ollama. Safe (**S**) and encounter (**E**) zones gain moment-to-moment mood without rewriting level geometry.

Trade-offs: small models occasionally break character or repeat phrasing; memory of bad replies required sanitization during development. The static quest panel remains the source of truth for objectives, so gameplay fairness is preserved.

## 6. Development workflow

AI-assisted tools accelerated implementation but did not replace design ownership:

| Tool | Role |
|---|---|
| **Cursor + Claude / GPT** | C# gameplay, UI fixes, documentation, NDJSON streaming debug |
| **Ollama (runtime)** | Playtest dialogue and flavor |
| **Meshy AI** | Cap and enemy meshes |
| **Pillow script** | Tileable dungeon albedos |

Every meaningful change is logged in `docs/refinements-changes.md` with date, rationale, and tool credit — satisfying auditability for academic submission. Prompt experiments are archived in `docs/prompts-used.md` and `Assets/DialogueOutput/ollama-dialogue.json`.

## 7. Conclusion

DungeonExporer demonstrates a **pragmatic local LLM integration**: tight scopes, authored quest authority, streaming UI, health checks, and player opt-out. The architecture is intentionally simple (HTTP generate + sanitize) so evaluators can trace data flow in a short technical video. Consolidating on SimpleOllamaUnity, adding warm-up, and expanding evaluation sets are the natural next steps beyond this vertical slice.
